ranked_movies.title: About Time
ranked_movies.reason: Explicitly mentioned as a bittersweet movie that deeply impacted a user, sparking a realization about the preciousness of time and family relationships.

ranked_movies.title: I Saw the Devil
ranked_movies.reason: Given as the prime example in a post asking for movies that get progressively more depressing and messed up, with a bleak ending.

ranked_movies.title: Requiem for a Dream
ranked_movies.reason: Top-voted comment in the 'depressing movies' thread, described as the best movie you'll only want to watch once due to its intensity.

ranked_movies.title: Grave of the Fireflies
ranked_movies.reason: Highly discussed as an extremely depressing movie that offers no happiness and leaves viewers destroyed.

ranked_movies.title: Oldboy
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently recommended in the same thread as 'I Saw the Devil' and 'Requiem for a Dream' for its dark and twisted narrative.

ranked_movies.title: Come and See
ranked_movies.reason: Specifically called out with its year (1985) as a quintessential and harrowing depressing war film.

ranked_movies.title: The Road
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended for its consistently downward trajectory and ambiguous, but likely bleak, ending.

ranked_movies.title: Parasite
ranked_movies.reason: Listed among the top depressing movies for its social commentary and tragic turns.

ranked_movies.title: Manchester by the Sea
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned with a simple 'Oof', indicating its recognition as a profoundly sad film about grief.

ranked_movies.title: Leaving Las Vegas
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended in the list of progressively depressing films.

ranked_movies.title: Eden Lake
ranked_movies.reason: Included in the list of movies that are dark and messed up.

ranked_movies.title: Children of Men
ranked_movies.reason: Suggested as a movie that fits the theme of being dark and depressing.

ranked_movies.title: Thirteen
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned as a film that is particularly shocking and depressing, especially upon realizing the characters' ages.

ranked_movies.title: Dancer in the Dark
ranked_movies.reason: Listed alongside Parasite as a famously bleak and tragic movie.

ranked_movies.title: Mary and Max
ranked_movies.reason: The central example in a post about unexpectedly dark movies; described as layering one bummer on top of another.

ranked_movies.title: Friendship
ranked_movies.reason: The other central example in the same post, noted for its tragic core of loneliness and failure to connect despite being a comedy.

ranked_movies.title: Groundhog Day
ranked_movies.reason: The subject of a 'Bad Movie Description' post that labeled it a 'depressing movie about adulthood,' highlighting its deeper, relatable themes within a comedy.

ranked_movies.title: Soul
ranked_movies.reason: Discussed as the last 'genuinely great' Pixar film that deals with deep, existential themes of life's purpose.

ranked_movies.title: The Brave Little Toaster
ranked_movies.reason: Cited as a movie that unexpectedly traumatized a generation of children who assumed it was a normal kids' film.

ranked_movies.title: Wall Street
ranked_movies.reason: Discussed in a historical context where its anti-capitalist message backfired, instead making audiences admire the luxury and excess it portrayed.
